dataList.content: ... Mm. No, wait—for this one, we should go about it like...
No, Heathcliff's been in the vanguard for too long. I should pull him back and let him—
Dante.
<... Huh? Oh, sorry. Did you say something?>
I provided you an answer to your question... but you exhibited no observable response.
Is the Sinners' combat performance deteriorating beyond manageable parameters?
that bad, but...>
<It's just hard to focus on one conversation when I'm trying to support battles happening in completely different places at once.>
<On top of that, Rodya's shopping chatter's starting to get a little distracting...>
A few days after we entered N Corp.'s District,
the Bus pulled over in the Backstreets of N Corp. to take care of a Distortion case.
This contract was supposed to be handled jointly with the LCD team, but...

Distortions suddenly started cropping up all over an area of the Backstreets called Butter Street, without any coherent pattern. No obvious point of origin or route of spread—nothing convenient like that, and I had no choice but to split the Sinners into several groups and send them out.
Outis, Ishmael, Sinclair, Don Quixote, Yi Sang, and Meursault were on their way to rendezvous with the LCD team.
Heathcliff, Gregor, and Hong Lu were suppressing Distortions that had showed up from other directions.
And with our onboard stock scraped down to the bottom, Rodya and Ryōshū, who were done with their share of the suppression work, were put in charge of buying supplies for the Bus.
As for Faust...

She decided to stay behind with me on the bus. I wanted to get through the consultation sessions I'd been putting off, and thought I might as well get myself some much-needed practice with handling the new Golden Bough power I'd acquired.
To reiterate: there are no issues requiring special concern regarding Faust.
It is true that Faust has, on several recent occasions, expressed dissatisfaction with a certain Faust. However, those instances should be classified as a mere personal grievance.
<I mean, that's good if that's all there is to it, but...>
This was not the first time Faust had seemed... unlike herself.
There had been times before when she spent far longer alone than usual, lost in thought—moments when she could not produce immediate answers to questions from the other Sinners or me.
<Could it be...>
<You can't access the Gesellschaft?>
... May I ask your basis for that inference?
<'Cause you're acting a lot like you did back on the WARP Train.>
... It appears there remain occasions on which you are still capable of surprising Faust, Dante.
<So I was right? You really are cut off from the Gesellschaft?!>
That was not intended as a confirmation of your conjecture.
Maybe she's saying that while she can technically access the Gesellschaft... it may as well be that she can't?
I wish I could ask more and get to the bottom of it, but the way she was sidestepping the subject told me that prying wouldn't give me any further insight.
<... Right. I won't press you if you don't wanna talk about it.>
... Your reserve is appreciated.
<Shall we move on?>
Yes. Your consultation is the next agenda.

After cross-referencing the records pertaining to the recent events in the House of Spiders, it would appear that memories concerning the individuals designated as "Sora" and "Ren"... currently remain only with you, Dante.
As such, should the influence of Ryōshū and her sword lead to further omissions in the official records, the Manager will be responsible for supplementing the necessary information.
<... Yeah. Not the first time I was the only one left with intact memories, huh.>
That's right. Despite K Corp.'s Memory Wipe Procedure, Catherine's "erasure" by the Golden Bough's power, and Arayashiki severing people from existence...
I was the only one who remembered what had transpired.
But ironically enough, the memories I had prior to swapping my head with this clock still showed no sign of returning.
<Why won't my old memories come back, though? Is it because of this "Clockhead"?>
Speculation based on your experience with the Bus Team thus far will not meaningfully contribute to the recovery of your former memories, Manager.
Inductive reasoning conducted from memories with gaps, after all, is bound to have structural limitations. Bongy would serve as an adequate analogy.

Yes, Bongy. Receiving feed and increasing in body mass alone would provide it with no meaningful basis for identifying the purpose of its rearing. Even at the terminal stage, when exposed to kitchen instruments and the heat of the stove, its limited experience would prove inadequate to constitute an understanding of its ultimate fate and purpose.

... Likewise, the ultimate answer to your doubts can only be obtained from the whole of the experience that you will have accumulated at the terminus of your entire journey—that is, your allotted mission.
<... Because treasure awaits at the end of the road. At the end of hell.>
<And that treasure will give me back my memories and my head. Also...>
Indeed. At the end of the road, you will be able to engrave your Aspect.
I still have no idea exactly what "Aspect" means, or even whether it's an object, but if I know one thing...
It's that the "me" from before all of this began had wanted it badly. Obsessively, desperately.
It's more like an instinctive knowledge... But with my memories gone, that feeling, however strong it is, does not change the fact that it's just another word with no discernible mental image.
<... And you're not gonna tell me what it actually is, are you? The Aspect.>
<What it means... or why I wanted it so much.>
If it remains a piece of information that must be disclosed to you, then no,
I am unable to tell you.
Pay it no mind. You are still following your Star acceptably. Please concentrate on the journey.
More importantly... Have you grown accustomed to that incomplete power? Current observation suggests you have yet to arrive at that level.
<Well, I've gotten pretty used to focusing on one place at a time... but keeping track of several places at once is still a bit rough.>
Outis's team is doing well in combat even without my direct supervision, and Rodya's team is shopping and not engaged in battle at the moment. Taking my mind off them would prove much easier...
But that wouldn't give me the much-needed practice, so I'm doing my best to keep an eye on every Sinner's status.
Sufficient familiarity should result in substantial tactical value. You would be able to cast a wider net when information-gathering and employ previously unavailable tactical patterns.
<Right. And I'd also be able to tell when it's a bad time to call someone in, too...>
Even as I continue talking with Faust, I make sure not to stop observing the places where each group is and what they're doing.
Yup, pulling Heathcliff to the rear was the right call. I don't see any injuries—his team's still doing pretty well even without my directions.
As for Rodya... Wait. Did she just stuff all of the pastries and bread she bought into her pockets? How did she manage that with a huge baguette...?
And Outis's group... Let's see. Ah, looks like they joined up with the LCD team without any issues.
LCD... Then that guy over there must be...
Hang on.
<Faust. There's a dude I've seen before, but... he seems a bit... uh, different from what I remember.>
... Could you specify the issue?
<I'm monitoring the situation right now. We should probably join up with them too.>
<Let's end the consultation here.>
This is rather precipitous, Dante. If the incident is of a serious nature, perhaps a request for support from the LCC—
<No, it's not an incident.>
... Though I wouldn't say it's not serious.
